title: Malaysia NGAIGE ↔ International Frameworks
description: Maps Malaysia's 7 NGAIGE principles to corresponding provisions in EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, and other international frameworks.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 10 requires training/validation/testing data to be representative, free from biases, and subject to data governance practices.

nist-ai-rmf.summary: MAP-2.3 covers fairness across protected characteristics. MEASURE-2.6 addresses bias testing and measurement.

iso-42001.summary: A.7.5 addresses bias mitigation in AI training data and model outputs.

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Fairness and non-discrimination in AI outcomes.

unesco-ai-ethics.summary: Core value of ensuring AI does not discriminate or create unfair outcomes.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 9 (risk management system), Art. 14 (human oversight), Art. 15 (accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity).

nist-ai-rmf.summary: MEASURE functions cover testing and validation. MANAGE-2.2 addresses human oversight mechanisms.

iso-42001.summary: Risk assessment (6.1), operational planning (8.1), and system verification (A.6.2.3).

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Robustness, security, and safety throughout AI lifecycle.

unesco-ai-ethics.summary: Prevention of harm and ensuring AI systems function safely.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 10 (data governance including privacy). Art. 15 (cybersecurity requirements).

nist-ai-rmf.summary: MAP-2.3 covers data privacy considerations. MANAGE-2.3 addresses security testing.

iso-42001.summary: Data privacy (A.7.4), access control (A.8.1), security testing (A.8.2), data security (A.8.4).

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Security and privacy as part of AI robustness.

singapore-maigf.summary: Data governance and protection principles for AI systems.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 27 fundamental rights impact assessment considers impact on different groups.

nist-ai-rmf.summary: MAP-5 addresses societal impact including equitable access and participation.

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Inclusive growth, sustainable development, and well-being.

unesco-ai-ethics.summary: AI should be accessible and beneficial to all, not deepening inequalities.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 13 (information to deployers), Art. 50 (disclosure obligations), Art. 85 (right to explanation).

nist-ai-rmf.summary: Transparency and explainability throughout the AI lifecycle.

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Transparency and explainability of AI systems.

unesco-ai-ethics.summary: AI processes and decisions should be transparent and explainable.

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 16-17 (provider obligations and QMS), Art. 26 (deployer obligations).

nist-ai-rmf.summary: Governance structures, roles, and accountability mechanisms.

iso-42001.summary: Leadership commitment (5.1), roles (5.3), internal audit (9.2), management review (9.3).

oecd-ai-principles.summary: Accountability for proper functioning of AI systems.

singapore-maigf.summary: Clear roles and accountability for AI governance within organisations.

mappings.principleName: Pursuit of Human Benefit and Happiness

eu-ai-act.summary: Art. 14 (human oversight preserving autonomy), Art. 27 (fundamental rights impact assessment).

nist-ai-rmf.summary: Societal impact assessment and human oversight in governance.

iso-42001.summary: Human oversight (A.3.2), human rights (A.3.4), societal impact (A.3.5).

oecd-ai-principles.summary: AI for inclusive growth, sustainable development, and well-being.

unesco-ai-ethics.summary: Core values of human dignity, autonomy, and societal well-being.

alignmentLegend.strong: Direct correspondence with explicit requirements or provisions
alignmentLegend.moderate: Indirect or partial coverage through related provisions
